name: Le Van An
skills: ReactJS, NextJS, Java Spring Boot, TypeScript
bio: 4+ years of experience building scalable ReactJS, NextJS, and Java applications for ICON plc and Nokia. Skilled in Agile methodologies, CI/CD pipelines, and Jest-based testing — delivered a 20% performance improvement and resolved 300+ critical bugs across enterprise-grade platforms.

companies.description: sZTP application (Nokia) — certificate & ownership-voucher (OV) management platform.
job: Translated high-fidelity Figma designs into reusable React components, ensuring a consistent and responsive UI/UX across modules.
Defined OpenAPI specifications and database schema designs with cross-functional teams, establishing a single source of truth for the platform's service contracts.
Partnered with product owners and backend/frontend teams to refine API contracts and align technical requirements, streamlining integration and accelerating delivery.
Championed UX improvements grounded in user-behavior insights and implementation constraints, measurably raising overall product usability.
Engineered and maintained backend APIs to OpenAPI standards while shipping UI components faithful to design specs — owning features end-to-end as a full-stack contributor.

companies.description: Access Management (Nokia) — multi-domain SDN/NFV automation platform for telecom providers.
job: Spearheaded migration from AngularJS to ReactJS, modularizing 30+ components and maintaining 100% feature parity to minimise disruption.
Developed 8 core features and resolved 90+ bugs and 15 enhancement tickets through iterative sprints, boosting platform stability by 25%.
Architected complex UI components (trees, forms, tables, filters, search, CRUD) with optimised rendering logic, improving render performance by 30%.
Established automated CI/CD pipelines and GitLab best practices, managing 5 seamless releases and cutting deployment time by 50%.
Integrated WebSocket to enable real-time updates for feeds and alerts without page reloads.

companies.description: Healthcare System (ICON plc) — clinical-trial data reporting platform for 200+ pharmaceutical researchers.
job: Conducted code reviews and pair programming, mentored intern developers, and led tech-sharing sessions to raise team knowledge and collaboration.
Accelerated page-load times by 20% by eliminating redundant hover handlers in a third-party UI library.
Architected responsive interfaces — tables, forms, and reports — delivering seamless experiences across desktop and mobile.
Customised React Query with advanced TypeScript to reduce API boilerplate, improving maintainability and developer efficiency.

companies.description: Restaurant Management System (RMS) — web & mobile app streamlining inventory, orders, reservations, and billing.
job: Engineered NextJS front-end with server-side rendering and optimised API calls, improving page-load performance by 25%.
Designed user-centred wireframes and prototypes in Figma, boosting staff task-completion rates by 30%.
Mentored intern developers through weekly code reviews and best-practice workshops, raising code quality and reducing bugs by 20%.
Collaborated on DynamoDB schema and Spring JPA integration for inventory/reservations modules, cutting data-fetch latency by 20%.
Conducted business analysis and manual QA for stock-reporting features — gathered feedback, defined KPIs, and validated 50+ test cases for release readiness.

companies.description: Online Bookstore System — online bookstore platform.
job: Developed and maintained responsive user interfaces with ReactJS, translating Figma designs into reusable components that accelerate feature delivery.
Collaborated with senior developers and the UX/UI team to implement enhancements and resolve UI bugs, ensuring cross-browser compatibility and a seamless experience.
Participated in Agile/Scrum ceremonies — daily stand-ups, sprint planning, and code reviews — gaining hands-on experience with Git workflow and team practices.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
